atomic_facts.fact: The text describes Cysticerci creating cavities in the brain and growing into tapeworms.
The text mentions tapeworms can be two to seven meters in length and live up to 25 years.
The text states that on a CT scan, cysticerci appear 'similar to a starry sky'.
The phrase 'similar to a starry sky' is highlighted in yellow.
In the second frame, the right side of the text box appears visually shifted or distorted compared to the first frame.

ocr: Cysticerci create cavities in the brain and other body tissue where their tiny bodies grow sometimes into tapeworms two to seven meters in length and can live up to 25 years in the human body. On magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) wormholes appear. Often times, if best practice is followed, the patient's entire body is scanned and more craters appear. Some authors have stated that cysticerci on a computed topography (CT) scan are similar to a starry sky or fireflies (Kapur et al. 2007).

atomic_facts.fact: The document title is 'Public Health Implications of Cysticercosis Acquired in the United States'.
The authors listed are Frank Sorvillo, Patricia Wilkins, Shira Shafir, and Mark Eberhard.
The text defines Neurocysticercosis (NCC) as an infection of the central nervous system by cysticerci from the pork tapeworm, Taenia solium.
The text states NCC can cause seizures, headaches, and focal neurologic deficits.
The text states NCC can cause sudden death due to high pressure on the brain and spinal cord.
A highlighted sentence fragment reads 'Cysticercosis has emerged as a cause of severe neurologic disease in the United States'.

ocr: Emerging Infectious Diseases • www.cdc.gov/eid • Vol. 17, No. 1, January 2011
Public Health Implications of Cysticercosis Acquired in the United States
Frank Sorvillo, Patricia Wilkins, Shira Shafir, and Mark Eberhard

Neurocysticercosis (NCC) occurs when the cysticerci from the pork tapeworm, Taenia solium, infect the central nervous system. Some people may remain asymptomatic their entire lives as carriers, while others are asymptomatic for a few years then suddenly become very ill with seizures, headaches, and other focal neurologic deficits as the cysticerci multiply within the nervous system, muscles, and other tissue. NCC can cause sudden death due to high amounts of pressure on the brain and spinal cord.
Cysticercosis has emerged as a cause of severe neurologic disease in the United States that primarily affects
1954–2005. A confirmed or presumptive source of infection was identified among household members or close personal
